message: The hero image feels a bit dark on my laptop screen. Can we lighten the overlay by 10%?
Done - overlay opacity reduced from 40% to 28%. Tagline contrast is still WCAG AA compliant.
Newsletter form copy should be 'Prenumerera på nyhetsbrevet', not 'Sign up'. This is a Swedish-first site.
Author bio block: please use the portrait photo Maja took in March, not a stock image.
Got it - which portrait for the Nora Clement event? Maja sent three options.
Adding a FR (French) language badge variant - Lucia has a small French shelf so we should be consistent.
The primary button colour (dark green) is right - matches the shop's interior paint. Keep it.
I will shoot the anniversary window display on 10 Oct. Frame draft looks good - leaving space for the anniversary banner along the top shelf.
Confirmed. I want Marta Kowalska's latest and Anders Lilja's backlist at the front. Will arrange the physical shelf this weekend.
The date on the poster reads '17 oktober 2026' - perfect. Can we add the shop address in small text at the bottom? Svartbäcksgatan 12, Uppsala.
Added address in 9pt Garamond at the bottom - matches the typography system. Also added weaverandbook.se URL.
Pre-order button must say 'Förboka' not 'Pre-order'. Everything customer-facing should be Swedish.
Updated all button labels across the shop template. 'Lägg i kundvagn', 'Förboka', 'Köp biljett' are now consistent.
About page draft text is in the Notion workspace. Jonas please pull it in - the 15th anniversary paragraph needs updating before print.
Final poster image delivered to the shared design folder: sofia-nyman-poster-final.tif. 300 DPI, CMYK, A3.
Summer reading theme: light linen background, pastel colours. Shelf should feature the French titles and Nordic non-fiction. Avoid heavy winter palette.
First batch of 8 staff picks entered. Each has Language badge and a short note in Swedish. Looks good - can we see the mobile grid layout?
Mobile grid preview attached. 2-column layout at 375px, 3-column at 768px. Pagination every 12 cards.
The astronomy draft turned out beautifully - glad we used the NASA Hubble imagery for the backdrop. Printing A1 for the window.
Nora Clement poster: font size on the date is too small. Reading nights often get last-minute attendees looking at the window - date must be legible from the street.
